channel.title: Parmjeet kaur Profile
channel.description: Recent content on Parmjeet kaur Profile

item.title: Bias and Fairness in AI
item.description:
Bias and Fairness in AI

Why AI Fairness Matters?

Artificial Intelligence has become a notable section nowadays. AI systems are promptly used in different sectors, such as healthcare, education, banking, employment, and law enforcement. These systems assist organizations in analyzing great volumes of information, automating tasks, and making future predictions. While AI brings many benefits for human beings, it also raises important ethical concerns.

One of the major issues is biasness in AI, where AI methods fabricate unfair or discriminatory results. Even though AI systems are often seen as objective, they learn from data created by humans. If this data reflects existing social inequalities or unfair patterns, AI might repeat or even worsen these problems.

item.title: The Ethics Behind Course Evaluation Surveys
item.description:
Introduction

Course evaluation surveys are commonly used by colleges and universities to gather feedback from students and improve educational programs. While these surveys seem straightforward, they can raise important ethical concerns when viewed from a data science perspective. Ethical data collection requires organizations to be transparent, protect privacy, obtain informed consent, and ensure fairness in how information is gathered and used.

In this scenario, students complete a ten-question survey at the end of a course. To access the survey, they must log in using their organizational username and password. The survey states that responses will remain anonymous and does not ask for the student’s name. Executive management intends to use the survey results to determine which courses may need redesigning or updating.
